title: Why was Misato at the South Pole
display_title: Why was Misato at the South Pole
wikitext:
[]: Why was Misato at the South Pole?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Aug 31, 2004 6:47 pm One of my favorite unsolved EVA questions is why was Misato at the South Pole for Second Impact. After all, people don't normally take their 14 year old daughters along on highly dangerous and TOP SECRET missions. Of course there's always the cynical answer that it was neccessary to the plot, which is true of any unlikely occurance, but the question is is there also some plot specific reason that we could figure out if we put our minds to it? For instance: 1: Could Prof. Katsuragi have been planning a contact experiment with Misato and Adam? 2: Were they already thinking of cloning an EVA from Adam and using Misato as it's pilot? 3: Or, if you want to get really far out, it is suspected that Kaworu was born on the day of 2I from Human DNA placed on the Lance and stuck into Adam. Could Kaworu be partially a clone of Misato? I doubt this last theory, (The Misato part of it that is; it's likely that whatever they did to Adam that day did result in Kaworu), but I can only think that they took Misato along for some nefarious reason. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Aug 31, 2004 7:32 pm Perhaps her father thought it was a simple scientific expedition, IE he had no idea what would happen.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 31, 2004 8:51 pm I've never liked the belief that Adam was awoken as a means of minimizing damage. I've always prefered believing that Adam awoke during tests and observations, and that they somehow in response managed to force him to expend his energy and revert to a dormant state. The events that led to Second Impact are a bit sketchy, but I like the idea that they tried to make an Eva from him. This could have been in one of two ways. 1) The division method (such as how Eva Unit 01 was spawned from Lilith) 2) Grafting the restraints and machinery onto Adam. After all, the Evas are not robots in the tradional sense, they are mostly biological. They have mechanical armor, and some additional mechanical structure for operation. It would probably be easier (presuming adam didn't awake) to create an Eva from him rather than by creating a cloned body to convert. Kind of an odd theory, but perhaps those casings on Adam's shoulders were actually armament casings like that of the Evas. Perhaps he awoke as they were in the process.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Aug 31, 2004 8:58 pm I agree, they had put Eva-like restraints on Adam, but I don't think they were trying to pilot him. They were trying to harvest his power, his S2 organ, it makes sense in context of everything that was going on days prior to Second Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Tue Aug 31, 2004 9:01 pm It's like asking why did Misato take Touji and Kensuke with Shinji to pick Eva 02? If it's a top secret weapon why would she do that? To answer my own question, it's probably because they had already seen and been inside of an eva. Though the answer has nothing to do with the original question at hand (If somebody starts drawing parallels to both situations there wil be much death!), I trying to say that there was a lot of lax in the rules. Top Secret usually means, 'a secret from everybody else but the people who are included on mission and their friends/family'. I've got feeling though, that Doc. Katsuragi had no idea that Adam was going to do his freak out and blow up the world. I do like that theory that they were trying to use Adam as the original weapon against the angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Sep 01, 2004 3:44 pm Misato was brought along on the expedition for the same reason Shinji was aloud to be around for the accident with his mother. Extra background plot information for Gainex/Anno to use.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Sep 02, 2004 4:49 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: 2) Grafting the restraints and machinery onto Adam. After all, the Evas are not robots in the tradional sense, they are mostly biological. They have mechanical armor, and some additional mechanical structure for operation. It would probably be easier (presuming adam didn't awake) to create an Eva from him rather than by creating a cloned body to convert. Kind of an odd theory, but perhaps those casings on Adam's shoulders were actually armament casings like that of the Evas. Perhaps he awoke as they were in the process.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Sep 02, 2004 8:44 pm The scientists had no idea what SEELE and\or Gendo was planning regarding the angels and such, so the idea of controlling Adam as an Eva would not make sense to them, they would have questioned it. They believed that they were sent there to harvest Adam's energy source, his S2 organ. Of course, Gendo fled the scene long before then, as he knew the disastrous consequences that would take place.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Sep 02, 2004 10:58 pm Maybe it was Dr. Katsuragi's time for visitation rights, and he just couldn't miss his expedition, even for his daughter, so he brought her along. I once read a fanfiction that tried to make use of Misato's presence on the expedition. I think it contained some far-fetched thing about Misato being impregated and actually being the mother of Rei (I think). Weird shit, whatever it was; if only I could remember the title. Was it Eva-R? That thing was so damn long, I can't even remember what happened.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Sep 17, 2004 10:30 am it was garden of eva... and i think mr katsuragi was feeling a bit guilty about Misato's childhood and wanted to take her somewhere and be a father to her...

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Fri Sep 17, 2004 3:50 pm sadsadshinji wrote: it was garden of eva... and i think mr katsuragi was feeling a bit guilty about Misato's childhood and wanted to take her somewhere and be a father to her...

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Sep 21, 2004 12:52 am Technically, the Second Impact, and thus, presumably, the Katsuragi Expedition, occurred not at the South Pole, but on or near Mt. Markham (there are actually two Mt. Markhams in Antarctica, but where would EVA be without a little ambiguity). In as much as we want to play the plausibility card, the South Pole would make less sense as a place of secrets, if only because the area has had a permanent scientific base there since 1957. The mountains, on the other hand, are intriguing. You may remember the fuss some years ago when a Martian meteorite found in Antarctica was claimed to show fossil evidence of life. In fact, it was a Japanese expedition (in January of 1970, I believe) that discovered the first concentration of meteorites in Antarctica. The continent's mountain ranges are the best places to look for them, "catching" them like a strainer as the ice sheets the rocks fall onto gradually flow to the ocean over centuries. Even today, Japan (not the United States) has the leading research program in Antarctica to recover ancient space debris--Japan has collected over 10,000 samples, most of which are former asteroids but a small percentage of which have turned out to be pieces of Mars, or of our moon. In short, a perfect cover story for the Katsuragi Expedition. And as for Misato, I'd say the whole moral of EVA is that "take your child to work day" is a bad idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Tue Sep 21, 2004 12:57 am you have a nice point there Mr.Horn i want to look into it now (damn you somthing more to take out of my time ARRGGHHH) lol.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Sep 21, 2004 11:17 am Karma Burn wrote: I've got feeling though, that Doc. Katsuragi had no idea that Adam was going to do his freak out and blow up the world. I do like that theory that they were trying to use Adam as the original weapon against the angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Wed Sep 22, 2004 6:07 pm It is quite possible that Gainax sacrificed to a plot hole there to make up the rest of the story? Hmmmmmmm...A possiblity?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Sep 23, 2004 8:27 pm Carl Horn wrote: Technically, the Second Impact, and thus, presumably, the Katsuragi Expedition, occurred not at the South Pole, but on or near Mt. Markham (there are actually two Mt. Markhams in Antarctica, but where would EVA be without a little ambiguity).

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Sep 23, 2004 8:45 pm Dave wrote: Carl Horn wrote: Technically, the Second Impact, and thus, presumably, the Katsuragi Expedition, occurred not at the South Pole, but on or near Mt. Markham (there are actually two Mt. Markhams in Antarctica, but where would EVA be without a little ambiguity).

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Sep 27, 2004 1:04 pm Phaze wrote: Dave wrote: Carl Horn wrote: Technically, the Second Impact, and thus, presumably, the Katsuragi Expedition, occurred not at the South Pole, but on or near Mt. Markham (there are actually two Mt. Markhams in Antarctica, but where would EVA be without a little ambiguity).

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Mon Sep 27, 2004 1:07 pm You wouldn't per chance be referring to this screen cap, would you?? http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=282

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Sep 27, 2004 2:13 pm bp32 wrote: You wouldn't per chance be referring to this screen cap, would you?? http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=282
